body_txt: The hero is undercover or in the role of a Fake Defector . One of their friends has been captured. To test the hero's commitment and loyalty, the Big Bad hands them a gun and tells them to kill the captive. One of two things usually happens. If the gun is empty, they pull the trigger and nothing happens. They may then outright refuse, or if the Big Bad is actually in range they point the gun at them and nothing happens. In the latter case, their cover is pretty much blown. If the gun is loaded, the tested subject may try to shoot the Big Bad . Sometimes this works, but the enemy has often anticipated this. They're out of range, the gun won't work on them, the hero is attacked when they twitch wrong, the one administering the test is actually a Mook etc. Sometimes, the hero manages to Take a Third Option and dodge the test entirely. In darker works, the hero may see no other options and actually kill the captive. If the one administering this "test" makes a pretense (or better) of having a respect for life, they may commend the testee's reluctance to pull the trigger, since killing should never be easy . Note for the Yanks here that "mate" is used in the British slang meaning of "friend", not necessarily one's actual mate, though occasionally killing your spouse or lover is used as a particularly strong test. Often, rather than a close friend of any sort the captive used for the test is actually an innocent, or someone the hero wouldn't normally kill in cold blood. This is a subtrope of Allegiance Affirmation , and If You're So Evil, Eat This Kitten! . If they do kill the captive, this is often a stage in Bring Them Around — once the character has done something, it's harder for him to leave. Compare Involuntary Battle to the Death , Finish Him! , Deadly Graduation .
